ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER OF DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND CHASE HEATON M.D.

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA IS A CLOSE FRIEND OF HIS, BUT NEITHER THAT FRIENDSHIP NOR ANY OTHER MATTER CAUSES HIM TO BE OTHER THAN FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN DECIDING THIS CASE. DEFENDANTS CHASE HEATON AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL TORT IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF TAMMIE MOSLEY IS SUSTAINED WITH THREE MONTHS LEAVE TO AMEND. IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, MS. MOSLEY MAY FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN APRIL 15, 2019 WHICH ALLEGES ANY VIABLE CLAIM IN ADDITION TO HER FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE. MS. MOSLEY MAY WISH TO USE THE THREE MONTHS TO TRY TO RETAIN COUNSEL SO THAT SHE MAY ALLEGE CLAIMS ON BEHALF OF HER CHILDREN. MS. MOSLEY MAY WISH TO CONTACT THE BAR ASSOCIATION OF SAN FRANCISCO REGARDING RETENTION OF COUNSEL. IF MS. MOSLEY DOES NOT FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BY APRIL 15, 2019, THEN THE COMPLAINT WITHOUT THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION WILL BE THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT. FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (302/HK)

Plaintiff Tammie Mosley?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint To Add Claim For Punitive Damages; Declaration Of Peter Berman, M.D., Facs; Declaration Of Jordan Haber, M.D., Dacr, Facr; Declaration Of David Mayer, M.D., F.I.C.S.; Declaration Of Wykeisha L. Orr, Esq.; [Proposed] Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Wykeisha Orr for Plaintiff.
David Depolo for Defendants.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF TAMMIE MOSLEY'S Motion For Leave To Amend First Amended Complaint To Add Claim For Punitive Damages;

Plaintiff Tammie Mosely's motion under CCP 425.13 "for leave to amend first amended complaint to add claim for punitive damages" is denied as untimely.

A CCP 425.13 motion must be filed "within two years after the complaint or not less than nine months before the date the matter is first set for trial, whichever is earlier." (Id.; emphasis added.) The "date the matter is first set for trial" is the "trial date," not the "trial setting date." (Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 989, 992-993.) In our case, the first trial date set was March 9, 2020, so plaintiff's CCP 425.13 motion had to be filed by June 9, 2019. Yet, the pending motion was filed more than 14 months later, on August 12, 2020. Plaintiff points to a January 15, 2019 court order giving her until April 15, 2019 to file an amended complaint "which seeks punitive damages?that are not prohibited by CCP 425.13." Plaintiff did file an amended complaint on April 12, 2019, but agreed on May 1, 2019 to dismiss from it the only count alleged to allow punitive damages. (Depolo Dec. Ex. B.) Hence, plaintiff's August 12, 2020 motion "to add claim for punitive damages."
Plaintiff cites no authority giving a court discretion to waive the time bar of CCP 425.13.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

APPEARANCES: Atty. SONJA M. DAHL, ESQ. of Donnelly Nelson DePolo Murray & Efremsky (Tel: 925-287-8181) appearing for defendants The Regents of the University of California (sued herein as "UCSF Medical Center") and Chase Heaton, M.D.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The matter is not contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendants Chase Heaton and the Regents of the University of California's demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional tort in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tammie Mosley is sustained with three months leave to amend. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Mosley may file a first amended complaint no later than April 15, 2019 which alleges any viable claim in addition to her first cause of action for medical negligence. Ms. Mosley may wish to use the three months to try to retain counsel so that she may allege claims on behalf of her children. Ms. Mosley may wish to contact the Bar Association of San Francisco regarding retention of counsel. If Ms. Mosley does not file a first amended complaint by April 15, 2019, then the complaint without the second cause of action will be the operative complaint.

FORMAL ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
